merged_events.event_description: Diplomatic relations established when Caleb Cushing presented credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary to Chinese authorities, establishing a U.S. legation.

evidence.quote: Diplomatic relations were first established on or about June 16, 1844, when Caleb Cushing presented his credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary and Commissioner to the Chinese authorities in Macao.

merged_events.event_description: Diplomatic relations interrupted when the Manchu Emperor abdicated in favor of a provisional republican government; the U.S. withheld recognition pending establishment of a permanent government.

evidence.quote: Diplomatic relations were interrupted on February 12, 1912, when, as a result of the Chinese Revolution, the Manchu Emperor abdicated his throne in favor of a provisional republican government. The United States withheld recognition of this new provisional government while awaiting the organization of a permanent government.

merged_events.event_description: Diplomatic relations resumed when the U.S. Legation in Beijing communicated a message from President Wilson to President Yuan Shikai upon convening of the National Assembly for the new Republic of China.

evidence.quote: Diplomatic relations were resumed on May 2, 1913, when the Legation in Beijing communicated a message from President Woodrow Wilson to President Yuan Shikai of China upon the convening of the National Assembly for the new Republic of China.

merged_events.event_description: Diplomatic relations interrupted when the U.S. Legation informed the newly declared Provisional Government that the U.S. would maintain only 'de facto relations' pending establishment of a formal government.

evidence.quote: Diplomatic relations were interrupted on December 9, 1924, when the Legation in Beijing informed the newly declared Provisional Government of the Republic of China that the United States would maintain "de facto relations" with it, pending the establishment of a formal government.

merged_events.event_description: Diplomatic relations resumed with the Nationalist Government of the Republic of China when U.S. diplomatic representatives signed a tariff revision treaty with Nationalist officials, constituting full recognition.

evidence.quote: On July 25, 1928, following a period of governmental instability and the emergence of the Nationalist Government of the Republic of China, U.S. diplomatic representatives in China signed a tariff revision treaty with Nationalist officials. The Department of State declared that this act constituted full recognition of the Nationalist Government of China, with which diplomatic relations would be conducted.

merged_events.event_description: The U.S. Legation in Beijing was elevated to the level of Embassy.

evidence.quote: The Legation in Beijing was elevated to the level of Embassy on June 18, 1935.

merged_events.event_description: The United States opened the U.S. Liaison Office in Beijing to handle all matters in the U.S.-PRC relationship except strictly formal diplomatic aspects, as a step toward normalization with the PRC.

evidence.quote: on May 1, 1973, the United States opened the U.S. Liaison Office in Beijing to handle all matters in the U.S.-PRC relationship "except the strictly formal diplomatic aspects of the relationship."

merged_events.event_description: The U.S. Liaison Office in Beijing was converted to a full Embassy, and Leonard F. Woodcock was appointed Ambassador, following U.S. recognition of the PRC on January 1, 1979.

evidence.quote: The U.S. Liaison Office in Beijing was converted to an Embassy on March 1, 1979, and Leonard F. Woodcock, who had been head of the Liaison Office, was appointed Ambassador.

merged_events.event_description: Caleb Cushing, commissioned as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary, presented credentials to Chinese authorities, establishing the first U.S. diplomatic mission to China.

evidence.quote: Caleb Cushing, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1843-05-08. Credentials presented: 1844-06-12.

merged_events.event_description: Nelson Trusler Johnson was promoted from Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary, elevating the U.S. mission in China from a Legation to an Embassy.

evidence.quote: Nelson Trusler Johnson, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1929-12-16. Credentials presented: 1930-02-01. Promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary 1935-09-17. Note: Commissioned to China.

merged_events.event_description: David K. E. Bruce was appointed Chief of the U.S. Liaison Office in Beijing, establishing a liaison-level presence with the People's Republic of China while the Embassy remained at Taipei.

evidence.quote: David Kirkpatrick Este Bruce, Chief. Appointed: 1973-03-15. Credentials presented: 1973-05-14. Left post on 1974-09-25.

merged_events.event_description: The United States terminated diplomatic relations with the Republic of China (Taiwan) on January 1, 1979, closing Embassy Taipei on February 28, 1979, ending formal representation to the ROC government.

evidence.quote: The United States established diplomatic relations with the People's Republic of China, and terminated them with the Republic of China, on January 1, 1979. Embassy Taipei closed February 28, 1979.

matched.notes: RDCR (Event 0) gives 1844-06-16, matching CSV. POCOM (Event 8) gives 1844-06-12 for Cushing's credential presentation. Per source priority, RDCR's date is correct and CSV follows it.
Exact date match (1912-02-12). Manchu Emperor abdication; U.S. withheld recognition.
Exact date match (1913-05-02). Resumption of relations with Republic of China.
Exact date match (1924-12-09). Relations interrupted; U.S. maintained only de facto relations with provisional government.
Exact date match (1928-07-25). Recognition of Nationalist Government via tariff treaty signing.

RDCR (Event 5) gives 1935-06-18 for institutional elevation, matching CSV. POCOM (Event 9) gives 1935-09-17 for Nelson Johnson's promotion to Ambassador, which is a later personnel event. CSV correctly follows RDCR.

RDCR (Event 6) gives 1973-05-01 for opening of Liaison Office, matching CSV. POCOM (Event 10) gives 1973-05-14 for Bruce's credential presentation, a later personnel event. CSV correctly follows RDCR.
Exact date match (1979-03-01). Conversion of Liaison Office to Embassy.

missing_from_csv.event_description: The United States terminated diplomatic relations with the Republic of China (Taiwan) on January 1, 1979, and Embassy Taipei closed on February 28, 1979. This represents the formal end of U.S.-ROC diplomatic relations concurrent with U.S. recognition of the PRC.
missing_from_csv.notes: CITATION ERROR flagged on this event — treat with extra skepticism. However, the historical fact of U.S.-ROC relations terminating on 1979-01-01 is well established. This event is conceptually distinct from Row 7 (1949-10-01, None), which marks the PRC's establishment. If the CSV tracks the U.S.-ROC relationship as part of the China timeline, this event could represent the formal termination of that relationship. However, it may be intentionally omitted if the CSV treats the ROC/PRC transition as occurring at Row 7 and the 1979 events as solely about the PRC (Rows 8-9). Whether to add depends on the dataset's conceptual framework for handling the China/Taiwan split.

unsupported_in_sources.notes: No extracted event corresponds to 1949-10-01 with status 'None'. The CSV notes this is 'coded from date of PRC establishment.' The RDCR narrative likely discusses the U.S. non-recognition of the PRC and the continuation of relations with the ROC government (which relocated to Taiwan), but no extracted event explicitly marks 1949-10-01 as a diplomatic status change to 'None.' This is an interpretive coding decision by the researcher — the PRC was proclaimed on October 1, 1949, but the U.S. continued relations with the ROC. The event may reflect a conceptual break in the 'China' timeline rather than a formal diplomatic action on that date. The closest extracted event is Event 11 (1979-01-01, termination of ROC relations), which is a much later date.
